title: zenkerella perplexa
intro: Zenkerella perplexa, also known as the 'African tree with a unique trunk', is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. Traditionally, the bark of Zenkerella perplexa has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in West and Central Africa, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The bark is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve fever, cough, and other respiratory issues, as well as promoting wound healing and reducing pain. In terms of herbal preparations, the bark of Zenkerella perplexa can be made into a decoction, a tea, or a tincture, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions.
